samples.xml_description:
Implement a multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer, data_in will remain constant during the period when data_en is high, and data_en is high for at least 3 clk_b clock cycles. When the value of data_en is high, data can be synchronized. The data change frequency of data_in is very low. The change interval between two adjacent data is at least 10 clk_b clock cycles. Clock signal A. Clock signal B. Active-low reset signal A. Active-low reset signal B. Input data signal. Input enable signal. Output data signal. Idle state. Wait for enable signal. Synchronize data. Register to store input data. Register to store input enable signal. Control register to store the first enable signal. Control register to store the second enable signal.

Reset the data_reg and en_data_reg registers on the positive edge of clk_a or the negative edge of arstn. If arstn is low, data_reg is set to 0. If arstn is high, data_reg is updated with data_in. If data_en is high, en_data_reg is updated with data_en.

Reset the en_clap_one and en_clap_two registers on the positive edge of clk_b or the negative edge of brstn. If brstn is low, both registers are set to 0. If brstn is high, en_clap_one is updated with the previous value of en_data_reg, and en_clap_two is updated with the previous value of en_clap_one.

Assign the value of data_reg to dataout on the positive edge of clk_b or the negative edge of brstn. If brstn is low, dataout is set to 0. If brstn is high and en_clap_two is high, dataout is updated with data_reg. If en_clap_two is low, dataout is not updated.
